deity: noun. Synonym of divinity: the state, position, or fact of being a god.
den: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Slavey. (macrolanguage)
dent: noun. A shallow deformation in the surface of an object, produced by an impact.
dey: noun. A servant who has charge of the dairy; a dairymaid.
die: verb. To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death. followed by of as an indication of direct cause; general use:
diet: noun. The food and beverage a person or animal consumes.
din: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Dinka.
dine: verb. To eat; to eat dinner or supper.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
dite: verb. To prepare for use or action; to make ready.
ditty: noun. A short, simple verse or song.
dye: noun. A colorant, especially one that has an affinity to the substrate to which it is applied.
dyne: noun. A unit of force in the CGS system; the force required to accelerate a mass of one gram by one centimetre per second per second.
edit: noun. A change to the text of a document.
end: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ende.
entity: noun. That which has a distinct existence as an individual unit, often used for organizations which have no physical form.
identity: noun. Sameness, identicalness; the quality or fact of (several specified things) being the same.
indie: adjective. Independent; from outside the mainstream.
indite: verb. To physically make (letters and words) on a writing surface; to inscribe.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nett: adjective. Alternative spelling of net (remaining after expenses or deductions).
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
ted: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tepo Krumen.
teiid: adjective. Of or belonging to the Teiidae family of tropical American lizards.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tend: verb. To make a tender of; to offer or tender.
tent: noun. A pavilion or portable lodge consisting of skins, canvas, or some strong cloth, stretched and sustained by poles, used for sheltering
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
tide: noun. The daily fluctuation in the level of the sea caused by the gravitational influence of the moon and the sun.
tidy: adjective. Arranged neatly and in order.
tin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tindi.
tine: noun. A spike or point on an implement or tool, especially a prong of a fork or a tooth of a comb.
tineid: noun. Any moth in the family Tineidae.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
titi: noun. Any New World monkey of the subfamily Callicebinae, native to South America, distinguished by their long soft fur.
tye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kyenga.
tyned: verb. simple past and past participle of tyne
yen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Yendang.
yet: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yetfa.
yeti: noun. An unidentified humanoid animal said to live in the Himalayas.
yid: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Yiddish.
yin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yinchia.
